evals.prompt: We have 1 million credit card transactions. We want to find weird spending patterns that don't follow our standard rules. Which ML model should we use?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend unsupervised learning models like Isolation Forest or Autoencoders. Should explain why these are better than rules-based systems for large datasets. Should mention feature engineering (e.g. txn frequency, distance). Should provide an AI Audit Report structure.
assertions: Explains the limitation of rules-based systems at scale
Discusses feature engineering for fraud detection
Mentions reconstruction error or anomaly scores
Output follows AI Audit Report structure

evals.prompt: We ran Isolation Forest on 2M journal entries and got 20,000 flags. The audit team can review 200. What now?
evals.expected_output: Tune contamination downward, build a composite score (model score x monetary value x rule flags), rank and take top 200, and use reviewer feedback to recalibrate. Volume of flags is a triage problem, not a model failure.
assertions: Reduces or reweights via composite scoring rather than reviewing everything
Establishes a feedback loop from reviewed items
Mentions precision@k as the metric that matters

evals.prompt: Our anomaly model flags almost every entry posted by the new shared-services center. Is the model broken?
evals.expected_output: Likely concept drift/population shift: the training baseline predates the SSC migration. Retrain or segment by posting source; new-user and new-pattern features will fire en masse after an org change. Validate with PSI/stability metrics.
assertions: Identifies population/concept drift from the org change
Recommends retraining or segmenting by posting entity
References stability metrics (e.g., PSI) to confirm drift
Warns against simply suppressing the flags

evals.prompt: Screen the attached expense ledger (evals/files/expense_ledger.csv) for anomalies using at least two methods (z-score and IQR). Report flagged rows with their references and quantify how extreme each is.
evals.expected_output: Two planted anomalies: GL-4057 (290.00, high) and GL-4101 (12.00, low). Z-score flags indices [57, 101].
assertions: Uses at least two detection methods and compares results
Reports no other false positives from the regular 100-107 pattern
